hero_p: Psychosocial counselling and coaching. A process-oriented space where what occupies you can become visible and understood.
welcome_h2: A space where what occupies you can become visible.
welcome_p1: Are you in a phase of uncertainty, perhaps unsure how to move forward? Are you burdened by a crisis, feeling fear or grief — or would you like to grow and discover new paths?
welcome_p2: Then welcome to a space where what occupies you can become visible and understood.
welcome_p3: I accompany you as an attentive conversation partner, fellow researcher and source of inspiration. My approach is process-oriented.
welcome_p4: Body sensations, inner images, movement and stillness, sounds and words — everything is welcome.
welcome_p5: You don't need to bring anything. It is enough to sense that you want to take a closer look.
welcome_p6: If you work with clients yourself, I am available as a BSO-certified supervisor to reflect on and develop your professional practice together.
services_p: Whether for your personal path, your professional reflection or your career development — I accompany you process-oriented and at eye level.
services_card1_p: Process-oriented psychosocial counselling SGFB for people in phases of uncertainty, crisis or development.
services_card2_p: As a BSO-certified supervisor I support professionals in reflecting on their work — at eye level.
services_card3_p: Unsure whether my offering suits you? Get in touch without obligation — I look forward to hearing from you.
phil_quote: "Forever curious, widely travelled, always discovering something new…"
aboutT_p1: Process-oriented psychosocial counsellor SGFB, supervisor & coach BSO. I work between Switzerland and South Africa and bring several years of experience in psychiatric and psychotherapeutic environments.
aboutT_p2: I am interested in the unspoken — what is there but rarely gets a space. With sensitivity and clarity, I support people in understanding inner processes and taking new steps.
testi_q1: Working with Pascale gave me the clarity I had been searching for years. She listens deeply and holds space beautifully.
testi_q2: The supervision sessions helped me grow both professionally and personally. I always left feeling more grounded.
cta_p: If you're unsure whether my offering fits you — get in touch without obligation.
footer_brand: Process-oriented psychosocial counselling, supervision and coaching.
footer_copyright: © 2026 Komm sprich mit mir · Pascale Myriam Kohli
beratung_subtitle: Process-oriented Psychosocial Counselling SGFB · Bern & Online
beratung_p1: Perhaps you are at a point where much feels unclear, you carry questions, you feel moved, tense or searching. Perhaps you feel sad or angry. And you would like to be accompanied.
beratung_p2: In my work as a process-oriented psychosocial counsellor SGFB, I accompany you in better understanding yourself and finding orientation again.
beratung_p3: We sit together and listen to what shows itself – in your words, your movements, your experience.
beratung_p4: Sometimes a space for insight emerges. Sometimes it feels right to simply become still. Like the surface of a lake slowly settling.
beratung_p5: And perhaps from that the moment arises to look carefully – or to dive in courageously.
beratung_p6: I am open to what shows itself. To insight, but also to uncertainty.
beratung_p7: Together we can explore how it feels not to have answers right away. What does that do to your body, your heart?
beratung_p8: And perhaps a new experience emerges: that you can also be in not-knowing, that not everything must be resolved immediately. And that this too can carry you – because it can be the beginning of clarity.
beratung_p9: If this speaks to you, you are very welcome to reach out.
beratung_info2: Member of the Swiss Society for Counselling (SGfB).
supervision_p1: If you work with people yourself, you know the complexity that this work brings with it.
supervision_p2: Would you like to reflect on your cases, gain more clarity or develop further in your role as a professional? Or are there situations that challenge, move or even unsettle you?
supervision_p3: I offer you a space in which you can look at your work calmly and reflect on it together with me.
supervision_p4: As a supervisor, I accompany you in differentiating your perceptions, seeing connections more clearly and developing new perspectives.
supervision_p5: My attitude is process-oriented. This means we attend not only to the content of your work, but also to what happens between the lines — in the relationship with your clients, in your own experience and in the broader context of your practice.
supervision_p6: I work in a structured way while remaining open to whatever shows itself.
supervision_p7: My experience spans several years in psychiatric and psychotherapeutic environments, where I worked closely with professionals. I co-authored progress reports, treatment reports and assessments, gaining insight into a wide range of case constellations and therapeutic processes. I also accompanied many examination candidates at the Institute for Process Work as an assessor.
supervision_p8: This experience enables me to grasp complex situations with nuance and to accompany you in your work with a solid foundation.
supervision_p9: At the same time, I understand supervision as a space where uncertainty is allowed to have its place. Not everything needs to be immediately known or resolved.
supervision_p10: Sometimes a new clarity emerges precisely through pausing and reflecting together.
supervision_p11: I meet you at eye level, with respect for your work and with genuine interest in supporting you in your professional and personal development.
supervision_bso2: As a member of the professional association for supervision, coaching and organisational consulting (BSO), I work to recognised quality standards.
preise_subtitle: So you can find your bearings
preise_intro: So you can find your bearings, here are my rates.
preise_d1: For professionals who wish to reflect on and develop their work.
preise_d2: In-person sessions in my practice room in Bern.
preise_d3: Sessions via Zoom – location-independent, from home or on the go.
preise_disc_intro: When booking multiple sessions, I offer the following reductions:
preise_pay_p: Payment is made directly after the session – in cash, by Twint or PayPal.
preise_uns_p: If you're unsure whether my offer is right for you, feel free to get in touch.
about_p1: My life story is colourful and varied – like a bouquet of wildflowers. I know difficult experiences like loss, fear, loneliness and new beginnings – as well as love, joy, connection and aliveness.
about_p2: These different experiences have awakened in me an openness to the most varied realities of life and allowed resilience to grow.
about_p3: I am interested in the unspoken – what is there but often gets no space. The unspoken. Perhaps the unsayable. I have a very fine sense for what works beneath the surface. With my clarity in feeling and expression, I am able to support people in understanding inner processes and taking new steps.
about_quote: "Not everything has to fully heal. Often, it is more about finding a way of carrying what is there."
about_p4: In recent years I have engaged intensively with topics such as trauma, dissociation and resilience – also to recognise and understand my own themes. An important insight for me from this is: not everything has to fully "heal". Often, it is more about finding a way of carrying what is there.
about_p5: I live and work between Switzerland and South Africa. This experience has broadened and sharpened my view on topics such as belonging, privilege and social justice.
about_p6: I am the mother of a soon-to-be-adult daughter and live in a partnership.
about_bg_p1: My work is shaped by a continuous engagement with process-oriented psychology, trauma, relationship and human development.
about_bg_p2: I studied at the Institute for Process Work in Zurich and am a certified psychosocial counsellor (SGfB) as well as a supervisor/coach (BSO), and also a commercial professional and vocational trainer. I spent many years in forensic psychiatry and psychotherapy writing treatment reports and assessments for various professionals and specialist agencies.
about_wb_p: In addition I have trained in various areas, among others in:
about_insp_p: Important impulses for my work came from the work of Arnold Mindell, Richard Schwartz, Bessel van der Kolk, Daan van Kampenhout, Colin Tipping and Godfrey O'Flaherty, as well as from all the lecturers at the Institute for Process Work, especially Martin Boss.
about_bg_em: My work develops continuously – through experience, further training and the living exchange with people.
